summarization.a: The image displays molecular structures of various silicon precursors

data_extraction.a:
| Figure | Structure |
| --- | --- |
| a | SiH₂(NH₂)₂ |
| b | SiH₂(OH)₂ |
| c | SiH₄ |
| d | SiH₂Cl₂ |
| e | SiH₂(CH₃)₂ |
| f | SiH₂DMA₂ |
| g | SiH₂HFMA₂ |
| h | DIPAS |
| i | SiH₂EMA₂ |
| j | BDEAS |
| k | BTBAS |

a.question: Which precursors are commercially available?
a.answer: SiH2EMA2, BDEAS, BTBAS and DIPAS are commercially available amino-silane precursors that could be potentially used in the ALD of SiO2 and Si3N4

a.question: Which precursors are used to model aminosilanes?
a.answer: The molecules SiH2(NH2)2, SiH2DMA2 and SiH2HFMA2 were selected as model amino-silanes in order to observe the effect of changing R′ and R″ on the alkylamide functional groups

a.question: How are the molecular structures modeled/ optimized?
a.answer: The molecular structures for a selection of silicon precursors and the molecules (SiH2(NH2)2 and SiH2(OH)2 respectively) employed to model the silicon nitride and oxide surfaces were optimized using BP86/TZVPP
